distributionalSourceJumpLawStatus.label: distributional source jump law
distributionalSourceJumpLawStatus.reason: For a piecewise smooth K, the singular part of P^*K is obtained by applying the distributional product rule to the jumps of K,K',...,K^(7) at s0.

jumpMatrixTriangularStatus.label: jump matrix is triangular after reversing derivative order
jumpMatrixTriangularStatus.reason: The coefficient of the highest remaining delta derivative uses the highest available jump through the leading term a_8(s0)D^8K; lower operator coefficients only enter lower triangular positions.

jumpMatrixInvertibleStatus.label: jump map is invertible
jumpMatrixInvertibleStatus.reason: The triangular diagonal is a nonzero signed power of the leading trace coefficient a_8(s0).  Hence the map from jumps to local source jets is bijective.

interiorSourceSolvedStatus.label: interior adjoint source solved by jumps
interiorSourceSolvedStatus.reason: Every scalar source row ell(f)=sum_{q=0}^7 d_q f^(q)(s0) has a unique jump vector Delta(ell) with P^*K=ell as a distributional singular source.

formula.sourceDistribution: ell(f)=sum_q d_q f^(q)(s0) corresponds to sum_q (-1)^q d_q delta_s0^(q)
formula.piecewiseEquation: P^*K=0 on each side of s0 after the jump is imposed

formalProof: Write K=K_- 1_{s<s0}+K_+ 1_{s>s0}.  Distributional differentiation expresses D^nK as the regular sidewise derivatives plus delta derivatives weighted by the jumps of K,...,K^(n-1).
Collecting the coefficient of delta^(q) gives the displayed linear jump equation.
Order the equations by descending q.  The new unknown entering at each step is the next highest derivative jump, multiplied by a nonzero signed multiple of a_8(s0).
Therefore the jump matrix is triangular and invertible.
